subject: 📊 Daily Portfolio: ⚡ VERA FDA APPROVED +9% | ANET approaching $185 TP1

🚪 Exit Alerts
✅ VERA — TRUTAKNA APPROVED (Accelerated) · July 7, 2026
Drug: Atacicept (Trutakna™) · atacicept-vymj Indication: Primary IgA Nephropathy (IgAN) · adults at risk of progression
Approval type: ACCELERATED — not full. Confirmatory data required. Confirmatory event: ORIGIN 3 eGFR analysis planned Q3 2026 → potential full approval 2027
News reaction: +~9% on July 8 (~$43.73) per Benzinga/STAT Analyst range: $35–$110 · GS Buy/$90 · highest $110
Halal: ✅ Verified Musaffa AAOIFI · June 29, 2026
📐 Stop & TP Levels — Re-derived Post-Approval
Stop (updated): $38.00 — below the pre-PDUFA trading floor ($39–$42 consolidation zone Jun 29–Jul 6). Original stop $24.50 was a CRL gap target — now obsolete. New stop anchors to pre-approval support.
  Action: Move stop up to $38 on your broker. The CRL risk is gone; protect against approval-rally mean-reversion.
TP1: $55.00 — post-approval stabilization zone, analyst near-term cluster. Sell 4 shares.
  Gross: ($55.00 − $41.7755) × 4 = +$52.90 · Exit commission: −$3.00 · Net: +$49.90 ✓
TP2: $78.00 — analyst consensus midpoint ($68–$83 range). Sell remaining 4 shares.
  Gross: ($78.00 − $41.7755) × 4 = +$144.90 · Exit commission: −$3.00 · Net: +$141.90 ✓
After TP1: Move stop on remaining 4 shares to $42 (entry cost lock-in). Free ride to TP2 from there.
⚠️ New Risk — ORIGIN 3 confirmatory data (Q3 2026):
Accelerated approval requires verification of clinical benefit. If ORIGIN 3 eGFR analysis misses: FDA can withdraw accelerated approval. This is a new binary event on your VERA position — typically Q3 2026. Monitor for that readout date announcement. When announced, apply the same pre-PDUFA risk framework: size, stop, probability.
IgAN market: competes with Novartis (Fabhalta), Otsuka (sparsentan). Trutakna is first dual BAFF/APRIL inhibitor — differentiated mechanism. Commercial launch can begin immediately (accelerated approval = market-ready now).
⚠️ ANET — Arista Networks · Hold to TP1, Exit by July 25 ⚠️ WATCH
Stop: $135.00 — just below $136.42 real support (pre-breakout base, confirmed as level post-breach recovery)
⚠️ Stop was breached July 2 at $137.53. Option B in effect: hold to $185 TP1, new operational floor $135. Recent news shows stock recovered to ~$171–$177 — stop breach reversed.
TP1: $185.00 — sell all 1.27 shares · May 2026 swing high zone / analyst consensus cluster ($189–$200 targets)
  Gross: ($185.00 − $157.79) × 1.27 = +$34.56 · Exit commission: −$3.00 · Net: +$31.56 ✓
Deadline: Friday July 25, 2026 — 17 days. Q2 earnings ~August 5 — do not hold through earnings. Exit by July 25 regardless of price if TP1 not hit.
  At market July 25 (estimated ~$173): Gross: ($173 − $157.79) × 1.27 ≈ +$19.32 · Net: +$16.32 ✓ (still green, commission covered)
Halal: ⚠️ UNVERIFIED — verify on Musaffa before exit or re-entry. Likely halal (no debt issues, strong FCF).
Analyst targets: KeyBanc $200, BofA $200, Morgan Stanley $190 (all raised July 6-7) · 17 analysts Buy · consensus $189.71
Q2 guidance: $2.8B revenue, 62-63% gross margin, 46-47% operating margin · AI fabric target $3.5B FY2026
Risk score: 38/100 MODERATE
Valuation Risk 55 (premium but justified by 35% growth) · Growth Risk 35 (supply constraint noted but demand “exceptional”) · Financial Health 15 (strong FCF, minimal debt)
Action: Set price alert at $185. Exit on hit or at market July 25, whichever first.
⏳ Watchlist Status
❌ CAPR — Capricor Therapeutics HALAL FAIL — DO NOT ENTER
Drug: Deramiocel (CAP-1002) · DMD cardiomyopathy · New PDUFA: August 22, 2026 (FDA lifted prior CRL) · AdCom July 29 (21 days)
Halal screen result (Zoya, July 7, 2026): Interest income $2.2M / Revenue $22.3M = 9.9% → exceeds AAOIFI 5% threshold
Decision: Remove from active watchlist. Do not enter regardless of setup quality. Run portfolio-manager to update status.
Note: new FDA PDUFA August 22 is now confirmed per public filings — but halal screen overrides all entry decisions.
📁 VRDN — ARCHIVED (event passed)
Viridian Therapeutics · Lumvoa (veligrotug) for Thyroid Eye Disease · FDA APPROVED June 30, 2026. Entry missed (IBKR blocker). Stock +7.6-12% on approval. UBS $50 target. Archive this entry — no further action needed.
📁 VERA (watchlist) — ARCHIVED (position active)
This watchlist entry is stale. 8 shares held in positions[]. Track via active position card above. FDA approved July 7. Watchlist entry can be removed.
📡 Catalyst Plays — July 8, 2026
15 searches run. Gate analysis: Gate 1 = confirmed event ≤45 days · Gate 2 = market cap <$2B · Gate 3 = halal pre-screen
Dropped at screening: CORT (Relacorilant PDUFA July 11) — market cap ~$9.4B → Gate 2 FAIL · CELC (gedatolisib PDUFA July 17) — market cap ~$5.3B → Gate 2 FAIL · CAPR — Gate 3 FAIL (halal, see watchlist above)
OTLK — Outlook Therapeutics, Inc. 🌑 WATCH · Score 2/5
Catalyst: FDA PDUFA July 29, 2026 — 21 days · 🌑 SOON
Drug: ONS-5010 / Lytenava™ (bevacizumab-vikg) · Class 1 BLA resubmission
Indication: Wet age-related macular degeneration (nAMD) — first ophthalmic bevacizumab formulation
Market cap: Micro-cap (specific figure unconfirmed · IBKR offline) · Float: unverified
Background: This is a BLA resubmission after a prior Complete Response Letter. FDA has already concluded “substantial evidence of effectiveness” exists per acceptance letter. Class 1 review = limited scope, FDA focusing on specific deficiencies addressed.
Probability: Class 1 resubmission base rate ~70–80% (when FDA acknowledges efficacy established). Prior CRL risk was manufacturing/labeling, not efficacy. Risk: FDA finds new concerns.
Bull scenario: Approval → first ophthalmic bevacizumab → competes with Eylea, Lucentis on price. Micro-cap stock could 2–4x.
Bear scenario: Second CRL → stock likely -50% to -70%.
Smart money: No visible institutional signal found.
Halal: ⚠️ Unverified — pharmaceutical company, no obvious disqualifiers. Check Musaffa before entry.

💵 If entering: ~$257 deployable · 30% cap ~$251 · Check Musaffa FIRST · Set stop at prior CRL base once you know the price. Say “analyze OTLK” for full 8-section deep dive before committing.
KPTI — Karyopharm Therapeutics ⬛ SKIP · No confirmed date
Catalyst: Phase 3 XPORT-EC-042 topline data “mid-2026” — no confirmed specific date
Drug: Selinexor (XPOVIO) maintenance in TP53 wild-type endometrial cancer
Market cap: ~$218M ✓ Gate 2 · Q1 2026 revenue: $35.1M (established commercial stage)
Gate 1 status: FAILS — no confirmed event date announced. “Mid-2026” is too vague for Gate 1.
⚠️ Data could drop any week. Watch for KPTI 8-K announcement of topline data. If confirmed ≤14 days, pre-score jumps to 4/5 → INVESTIGATE tier. Analyst 12-month target $13.50 (+42% from ~$9.62).
MSFT — Microsoft Corp · Tier A Fallback 📊 TIER A FALLBACK
No small-cap catalyst plays met all 3 gates today — Tier A applied per rotation-playbook.
Catalyst: Q4 FY2026 earnings (estimated July 28–30, 2026 — ~20 days · unconfirmed exact date)
Market cap: ~$3T — well above Gate 2 threshold. This is a large-cap anchor candidate, not a catalyst play.
Rationale: Azure AI demand, Copilot monetization, strong FCF (~$75B/yr). When ANET exits July 25, freed capital (~$220) could anchor a MSFT starter position.
Halal: ✅ Generally AAOIFI-compliant (software/services revenue dominant, minimal interest income). Verify on Musaffa before entry.
Conviction: 7/10 as a rotation target post-ANET exit. Not a binary event play.

Say “analyze MSFT” for full deep dive. Confirm Q4 earnings date before sizing — avoid holding through an earnings event at a starter position.
💵 Cash Deployment — $265.41 available
Available cash (from portfolio.json)$265.41
Minimum reserve (cover 1 exit commission)−$3.00
Buy commission (if deploying)−$3.00
Net deployable capital$259.41
30% concentration cap (est. portfolio ~$835)≤$250 per new position
💡 Recommendation: HOLD CASH
Option A (catalyst play): OTLK PDUFA July 29 — if Musaffa verifies halal, ~$250 position in OTLK is within cap. 21 days to event. Risk: prior CRL history, micro-cap volatility. Decision needed by ~July 22 (give yourself a week to monitor pre-PDUFA flow).
Option B (hold): Wait for ANET to exit at $185 (July 25). Combined freed capital (~$220 net) + current cash = ~$480+ deployable. Better to enter MSFT or next catalyst play at proper size.
Recommended: Option B — hold. VERA just resolved; ANET approaching exit. No urgency. Cash at $265 does nothing harmful. The worst rotation is a rushed one. Your call.
🔄 Rotation Plan (Sequenced)
Phase 1 — This week (VERA consolidation)
① Move VERA stop from $24.50 → $38 in IBKR. Old stop was a CRL gap target — irrelevant post-approval.
② Hold 8 shares toward TP1 $55. No trim until $55 is reached.
③ Set ANET price alert at $185. Keep hard exit date July 25.
④ Verify ANET halal status on Musaffa (unverified in portfolio.json).
Phase 2 — VERA TP1 hit ($55)
Sell 4 shares @ $55 · Gross +$52.90 · Net +$49.90
Proceeds ~$220 + existing $265.41 cash = ~$485 deployable
→ Redeploy ~$250 into MSFT (Tier A anchor) or next catalyst play that passes all 3 gates
→ Keep ~$235 as reserve for VERA runners stop management
Phase 3 — ANET TP1 exit (target July 25)
Sell 1.27 ANET shares at $185 or market July 25 · Net +$31.56 at TP1
Freed capital ($185 net) → add to next position or increase MSFT starter
Q2 earnings August 5 — do not let ANET linger past July 25 regardless
All rotations net-positive after $3 commission. No money-losing moves in this plan. Your call on timing.

No HIGH-risk rotation trigger today — VERA risk arises from new confirmatory event, not immediate thesis failure.
📚 Equity Education · Day 7 of 30 US equity concepts — applied to your portfolio
Free Cash Flow (FCF) — What the Business Actually Generates
FCF = Operating Cash Flow − Capital Expenditures
Unlike net income or EPS, FCF is hard to inflate: you either have cash in the bank or you don’t. That’s why analysts price asset-light businesses like ANET on FCF yield rather than P/E.
ANET (your holding, exits July 25):
ANET generates ~38–40% FCF margin on $10B+ annual revenue — roughly $4B in free cash per year. That’s why KeyBanc, BofA, and Morgan Stanley can put $190–$200 targets on a stock at 40x forward earnings: the FCF quality justifies the multiple. When Q2 earnings drop in August (after your exit), the number analysts will scrutinize isn’t EPS — it’s FCF conversion rate and whether margins expanded or compressed.

VERA (your other holding):
Right now: irrelevant. Pre-revenue biotechs burn cash and have negative FCF — that’s expected. The investment thesis is about future FCF potential, not current generation. Post-approval, watch for FCF inflection — the quarter where gross margin (likely 80%+ for a biologic) starts outrunning commercialization costs. That’s when VERA’s equity story moves from “binary catalyst” to “compounding cash machine.” Atacicept’s commercial launch starts now. That inflection is ~2–3 years out.

How it fits your exit decision:
FCF yield = FCF / Market Cap. For ANET at $185 (your TP1), FCF yield is roughly 4.5–5%. That’s above the S&P 500 average (~3%) for a high-growth company — reasonable, not stretched. Above $200, yield compresses to ~4% and the risk/reward tightens. TP1 at $185 is the right exit point from a FCF-yield perspective too.
Also today: FCF Yield = FCF / Market Cap → tells you the real return per dollar of equity. A 5% FCF yield on a growth stock = buy. A 2% FCF yield on a growth stock that’s decelerating = rethink. (FCF Part 2 — owner earnings — was already covered in a previous session.)
